rawContent.message: No previous snapshot found — first QA run

html.message: No previous snapshot found — full QA run

contentComparison.reason: First QA run — full checks executed

minHeight.detail: CSS rule `.post-hero-inner { min-height: min(700px, 100vh); }` confirmed in PostHero.css (line 3). Computed style at 1440px viewport: minHeight=700px, height=700px. At 375px viewport (height=900px): minHeight=700px, height=700px — correctly clamps to 700px since 700 < 900. The `min()` CSS function is applied correctly.

contentAlignment.detail: Content layer carries `mt-auto pt-[var(--header-height)]` in both TSX (line 145) and PHP (line 62). `.post-hero-inner` has `display: flex; flex-direction: column` (confirmed via computed styles). At mobile 375px the content top is 512px within a 700px container, placing it at the bottom as intended. Visual inspection at all breakpoints confirms content (topic pill, h1, author, date) sits at the bottom of the hero area.

breakpoints.375px: Hero renders correctly. Full-width layout with stacked content at bottom. No horizontal overflow. Topic pill, h1 title wraps gracefully to two lines ('TEST: Post / Hero'), author and date below. Background image with gradient mask visible.
breakpoints.768px: Tablet layout renders correctly. Slightly wider, content still bottom-aligned. All 4 variations visible and properly stacked. No layout breakage.
breakpoints.1024px: Laptop layout renders cleanly. Hero height at min-height constraint. Content left-aligned in max-w-[700px] container, positioned at bottom.
breakpoints.1440px: Desktop layout renders correctly. All 4 variations (default, with excerpt, topic color, full options) clearly distinguishable. Content bottom-aligned, gradient system visible. No overflow.

tsxPhpSync.detail: Content layer class is identical in both files: `relative z-10 pb-8 sm:pb-16 mt-auto pt-[var(--header-height)]` (TSX line 145, PHP line 62). All structural elements match: section wrapper, .top-gradient div, .post-hero-inner, background image div with mask styles, content layer, container, max-w-[700px], topic pills, h1, optional excerpt, author/date group.

build.detail: Build and TypeScript compilation succeeded. Lint warnings are pre-existing across other blocks (Accordion, Archive, EventHero, etc.) and are unrelated to PostHero CSS/layout changes. Two PostHero warnings (useSelect missing dependency) are pre-existing and do not affect the edited code paths.

lint.detail: Pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in PostHero.tsx (lines 72-73) for useSelect — not introduced by this edit, not related to layout/min-height changes.

registration.detail: taktician_validate_registration passed. block.json apiVersion, naming (takt/post-hero), file references, attribute types all valid.

tsxPhpSync.detail: taktician_validate_all sync check passed. phpExtractPattern: pass (no $attributes[] anti-pattern). phpDocblock: pass. attributeUsage: pass. contextConsistency: pass. The root wrapper mismatch warning (TSX InspectorControls vs PHP section) is a known false positive from the automated tool — TSX correctly wraps InspectorControls + section in a fragment, the actual rendered wrapper is <section> in both.
tsxPhpSync.toolWarning: htmlStructureSync warning about InspectorControls vs section root is a known false positive — TSX uses React fragment (<>) containing InspectorControls + section; the actual DOM root is <section> matching PHP.

attributes.detail: 4 block instances present: default (no attrs), showExcerpt:true, useTopicColor:true, showExcerpt+useTopicColor both true. All attribute combinations render without errors. page_audit confirms all 4 instances have content, images, and correct selectors present.

edgeCases.detail: All 4 instances render on the test post. No horizontal overflow at any breakpoint (automated check passed). No JS console errors. Image with alt='' on decorative background image is correct (role=presentation in PHP). Heading hierarchy passes (h1 in each hero).

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements — block is a static display block, no JS-driven state changes.

accessibility.detail: No WAI-ARIA pattern tests apply (no carousel, accordion, tabs, etc.). Heading hierarchy passes (automated). Images: background image uses alt='' with role=presentation (PHP), correct for decorative use. Semantic structure: section > h1 per block instance, correct landmark use. No patterns from a11yPatterns list (none returned by validate_block_rules).
accessibility.patternCompliance: skipped — no applicable WAI-ARIA patterns for a static hero block

markupDuplication.detail: No duplicated markup for responsive layouts. Single DOM structure used at all breakpoints — responsive behavior handled entirely via Tailwind utility classes.

specCompliance.detail: 4 block instances on test page matching all planned variations. All wrapped in takt/demo-container. No placeholder IDs found in raw content. Page returns HTTP 200. Block renders visible content in all instances.

manual.RESPONSIVE_RENDER: pass — verified via screenshots at 375, 768, 1024, 1440px across chromium/firefox/webkit
manual.DARK_THEME: n/a — block has no theme/color variant attribute (useTopicColor changes accent gradient color, not a dark/light theme variant)

passedChecks: min-height: min(700px, 100vh) applied to .post-hero-inner — confirmed via computed styles (700px at 1440px viewport, 700px at 375px/900px-tall viewport)
display: flex; flex-direction: column on .post-hero-inner confirmed
mt-auto on content layer pushes content to bottom — content top at 512px within 700px container at mobile
Content layer classes identical in TSX and PHP (mt-auto pt-[var(--header-height)] pb-8 sm:pb-16)
No horizontal overflow at any breakpoint
All 4 block attribute variations render correctly
12 final screenshots captured (3 browsers x 4 widths)
